skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Apps: Tavily Search, Tavily Extract, Exa Search, Exa Answer, Exa Extract. Capabilities: AI-powered search, content extraction, direct answers, research. Use for: research, RAG pipelines, fact-checking, content aggregation, agents. Triggers: web search, tavily, exa, search api, content extraction, research, internet search, ai search, search assistant, web scraping, rag, perplexity alternative
skill.summary: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Apps: Tavily Search, Tav...
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 41 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown documentation as executable code. External commands are documented CLI usage, network calls are intended API endpoints, and the cryptographic reference is legitimate checksum verification. This is a documentation skill with no actual security issues.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Documentation (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are bash command examples in markdown code blocks, not executable Ruby code. The skill is documentation describing CLI usage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code blocks contain bash commands like 'infsh app run', not Ruby backtick syntax. This is markdown documentation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded URLs but these are legitimate service endpoints (inference.sh, example.com) that the skill is designed to use. They are documentation for the API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to inference.sh service and example.com (placeholder). These are expected API endpoints for the skill.

low_findings.title: SHA-256 Reference (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but the text 'SHA-256' refers to checksum verification, which is a security best practice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly recommends SHA-256 checksum verification for security, not weak crypto.

low_findings.title: Shell Pipe Install Pattern (Documented Risk)
low_findings.description: The 'curl | sh' pattern at line 16 is standard CLI installation practice. The skill notes the script verifies SHA-256 checksums and provides manual verification options.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: While pipe-to-shell is inherently risky, the skill documentation addresses this with checksum verification guidance.

content.value_statement: This skill enables AI agents to search the web and extract content from URLs using Tavily and Exa AI-powered search APIs. It provides research capabilities for fact-checking, content aggregation, and building RAG pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Search the web using Tavily AI-powered search with generated answers
Extract clean text and images from multiple URLs
Search using Exa AI with smart relevance ranking
Get direct factual answers to questions via Exa Answer
Extract and analyze web page content for RAG pipelines
Combine search with LLM analysis for research workflows
limitations: Requires an inference.sh account and API authentication
Usage costs apply based on Tavily and Exa API calls
Cannot access paywalled or authenticated content
Extract performance varies with website structure

use_cases.title: Research Agent
use_cases.description: Build AI agents that can search the web for current information and cite sources

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking
use_cases.description: Verify claims by searching multiple sources and extracting relevant information

use_cases.title: RAG Data Preparation
use_cases.description: Extract content from URLs to build knowledge bases for retrieval-augmented generation

prompt_templates.title: Simple Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for information about {topic}

prompt_templates.title: Research with Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {topic} and provide a summary with sources

prompt_templates.title: Extract Content from URLs
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from these URLs: {urls}

prompt_templates.title: Research and Analyze
prompt_templates.prompt: First search for {topic}, then analyze the results to identify key trends

output_examples.input: Search for latest developments in quantum computing
output_examples.output: Based on recent searches, key developments in quantum computing include: 1) Error correction breakthroughs at MIT... [sources: nature.com, science.org]
Extracted content: Article discusses emerging AI frameworks. Key points: - New tooling approaches... - Performance improvements...

output_examples.input: What is the population of Tokyo?
output_examples.output: The population of Tokyo is approximately 37 million people in the greater metropolitan area.

best_practices: Use specific search queries for better results
Verify extracted content accuracy by checking multiple sources
Combine search results with LLM analysis for comprehensive research
anti_patterns: Using overly broad search queries that return irrelevant results
Assuming extracted content is always accurate without verification
Ignoring API rate limits and incurring unnecessary costs

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign up at inference.sh and authenticate with the CLI using infsh login.

faq.question: What search providers are available?
faq.answer: This skill supports Tavily (AI-powered search) and Exa (smart search with direct answers).

faq.question: Can I extract content from any website?
faq.answer: You can extract from most public websites. Paywalled or authenticated content may not be accessible.

faq.question: How much does it cost?
faq.answer: Costs depend on your inference.sh plan and API usage. Check inference.sh pricing for details.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial products?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can be used in commercial applications subject to inference.sh terms of service.

faq.question: Is this available in Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code tools.
